title: COMMUNITY MEDIATION PROGRAM - 211info
text: RESOLUTIONS NORTHWEST COMMUNITY MEDIATION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Helps community members resolve differences outside of the legal system. The program offers confidential and effective mediation services for families and youth, peer mediation training for youth in Portland Public Schools (PPS) and any other conflicts within Multnomah County. Includes a specific mediation program for landlords and tenants experiencing conflicts such as rent increases, rent past due, housing discrimination, noise, and accommodation needs that could otherwise lead to eviction. 4531 SE Belmont Street Suite 120 Portland, OR 97215 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm, evening and weekend hours by appointment Email (503) 595-4890 . Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers (503) 595-4890 Main phone Main phone (503) 595-4890 Landlord/Tenant Mediation Erion Moore [email protected] Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Email, call, or visit website for more information FEES: Varies by service DOCUMENTS: None required at intake LANGUAGES: English, interpretation or signing by arrangement AREA SERVED: Multnomah County TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: TriMet Line 15 with a stop at SE Belmont & 46th Streets Services: Landlord/Tenant Dispute Resolution Mediation If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. RESOLUTIONS NORTHWEST
